item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got two links to propose here. The first is making the mention of the "AVB (Anomalous Vehicle Bay)" a crosslink to Dr. Reach's Blueprint Folder which has a wonderful illustration of what that and many other containment facilities might look like, and will facilitate much wikiwalking. Additionally, someone mentioned up thread that the effect here has noteworthy similarity with SCP-1539, and its likely that SCPF personnel would look into that to see if the two effects are related. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1946 is contained at Site-116 in its AVB (Anomalous Vehicle Bay), on lot #1542/A. No personnel are to enter SCP-1946 unless testing procedures are underway, and only D-Class personnel are to be allowed access to SCP-1946 during these procedures. As repeated attempts have shown, SCP-1946's main anomalous effect manifests too rapidly for affected subjects to be retrieved, and leaves subjects in a state of physiological stasis. Therefore, any personnel used in testing of SCP-1946 is to be considered permanently attached to this project. As the available space inside SCP-1946 is both non-anomalous and finite, requests for testing are to be screened for feasibility. Exploration of SCP-1946, if needed, is to be performed using robotic reconnaissance equipment.

To this:

SCP-1946 is contained at Site-116 in its AVB (Anomalous Vehicle Bay), on lot #1542/A. No personnel are to enter SCP-1946 unless testing procedures are underway, and only D-Class personnel are to be allowed access to SCP-1946 during these procedures. As repeated attempts have shown, SCP-1946's main anomalous effect manifests too rapidly for affected subjects to be retrieved, and leaves subjects in a state of physiological stasis. Therefore, any personnel used in testing of SCP-1946 is to be considered permanently attached to this project. As the available space inside SCP-1946 is both non-anomalous and finite, requests for testing are to be screened for feasibility. Exploration of SCP-1946, if needed, is to be performed using robotic reconnaissance equipment.

Changing this:

SCP-1946's anomalous effect manifests itself when a human being is introduced to its interior. Within approximately 3 minutes of exposure to SCP-1946, subjects will begin to lose any previously established identity and personality, and begin imitating elements of SCP-1946's interior. This process rapidly completes, with the longest cycle from start to finish taking approximately 2 minutes. Subjects first become agitated and confused, before gradually losing all ability to form coherent speech. However, an affected subject does not lose the basic ability to produce sounds. Multiple subjects have been recorded vocalizing in an attempt to more closely imitate an element of SCP-1946 (please refer to addendum 1946-A-01 for examples.) Attempts to communicate verbally with subjects after completion of the anomalous process have yielded no results. However, reactions can be provoked from subjects imitating elements with (digital or analog) controls using remote manipulation equipment. Subjects affected by SCP-1946 appear to enter a state of physiological stasis, do not appear to need sustenance, and have not been shown to undergo normal physiological processes.

To this:

SCP-1946's anomalous effect manifests itself when a human being is introduced to its interior. Within approximately 3 minutes of exposure to SCP-1946, subjects will begin to lose any previously established identity and personality, and begin imitating elements of SCP-1946's interior.1 This process rapidly completes, with the longest cycle from start to finish taking approximately 2 minutes. Subjects first become agitated and confused, before gradually losing all ability to form coherent speech. However, an affected subject does not lose the basic ability to produce sounds. Multiple subjects have been recorded vocalizing in an attempt to more closely imitate an element of SCP-1946 (please refer to addendum 1946-A-01 for examples.) Attempts to communicate verbally with subjects after completion of the anomalous process have yielded no results. However, reactions can be provoked from subjects imitating elements with (digital or analog) controls using remote manipulation equipment. Subjects affected by SCP-1946 appear to enter a state of physiological stasis, do not appear to need sustenance, and have not been shown to undergo normal physiological processes.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Investigation into whether this effect is a variation of the one observed in SCP-1539 or visa versa is under investigation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm torn on this one. The end game of people acting like dishwashers and other common household objects is more silly than "scary/weird". The idea of watching someone become "agitated and confused" as their entire psyche is crushed, leaving behind only a chemical toilet? For some reason that's sticking with me.

No vote, but I wanted to ask… anybody think this place might be missing a toaster?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damnit this is great +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not at all. I suppose it means the article does what it's supposed to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it bad that I found this pretty funny? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for kinda making that seem plausible. When you write stuff up in an hour, some things fall by the wayside. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The classic hangman's rule of thumb is that it takes 1000 pounds of force to reliably snap a neck. Which is a bit more than I can see the neck and shoulder muscles bringing to bear.

But that figure is for a short, sharp, 1 time jolt, and allows for a large "safety" margin to include a range of body weights. Smaller forces can be just as catastrophic, if repeated frequently for a long enough time. Factor in the possibility of a pre-weakened spine, maybe osteoporosis or an old football inury, and it's just barely plausible to Linda Blair oneself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this but the thing about twisting the neck until it fractures is silly. Like…how do you do that by yourself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: happy to be of service! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvote. I laughed, and it was a bit creepy, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really well done! Very much liked the humorous aspects of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the hell. XD

I like that the register dude "reproduces" coins. Geez. +1 for Frequent Boeing Foot Noodles if nothing else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is, though it might not be a euphemism for what you think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually dislike that exungement quite a bit, but this all turned out rather well.

Also:

Has attempted to interface with SCP-1946's supply of gas canisters.

Is that a euphamism?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better than the draft, also, that expungement cracks me up in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, they should. Thanks for catching that. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow… this is weird. In a good way.

All but SCP-1946-8 remain inside SCP-1946. SCP-1946-7 is deceased, and was examined to determine potential alterations to subjects' physiology. None were found, though SCP-1946-7's remains have not been noted to undergo decomposition since the subject expired.

Should all of these be -8?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well, for once, a data redacted that makes me glad it's there. not sure I wanted to know what a chemical toilet human would do anyway. The addendum pulls the feel of this skip together for me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked how you organized the addendum, it started out funny but ended with cringes.
Dammit Crayne I would have looked at this if I didn't get on chat so late

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral for now.

I'm mixed and I have to think about this. On the one hand, it seems like typical identity collapse, something that I think we can all agree the the toaster that is me and the identity-mixing laundry room does better. On the other hand, I liked a lot of the mental images from the addendum and I personally don't think its bad to have similar skips that may share a common origin/effect. I'll think about it and come back. Plus, its absolutely great for a one-hour skip.

EDIT: Eh, what the hell, +1 for redacting dash eleven.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. I'm for it. This is gloriously weird and disturbing. Identity fuckery always seems to skeeve me out, no matter how ridiculous.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reworded it, hope that helps. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep that aspect in there, but change the wording to be less redundant. Would give suggestions, but I gotta jet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's true. I just liked the idea of the trailer slowly filling up with people trying to be salt shakers, ketchup bottles and packets of splenda, and wanted to hint at that image. Do you think I should remove that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty cool, but I can't help but think that it doesn't need to be said that subjects reduce usable space by their volume — not only because it's obvious, but because in a sense, there ISN'T any usable space in the trailer to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty cool. An upvote for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an answer to Bright's "Write a scip in 1 hour, get feedback in 24 hours and post" challenge. Enjoy and let me know if something needs to be addressed.
